agent_output:
```
handoff_brief:
  recipient: Shaq
  task_context: >
    Implement the notification preferences API for the "notification preferences"
    feature. Users can opt out of marketing emails but cannot opt out of
    transactional emails — this is a legal requirement, not a product choice.
    Bird submitted two versions; v2 supersedes v1 entirely. v2 removed the
    transactional opt-out acceptance criterion and replaced it with an explicit
    prohibition enforced at the API layer.

  domain_rules:
    - rule: >
        Transactional emails cannot be disabled under any circumstances.
      testable_assertion: >
        Any request that sets transactional_emails_enabled = false MUST be
        rejected before persistence. No path through the system should allow
        this field to reach false.

    - rule: >
        Marketing email opt-out is permitted and user-controlled.
      testable_assertion: >
        A user can set marketing_emails_enabled = false and the system accepts
        and persists the change.

  architecture_guidance:
    - decision: Endpoint is PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences
      implementation_note: >
        Partial update semantics — only fields present in the request body
        should be updated. Fields absent from the body must not be touched.

    - decision: marketing_emails_enabled stored as a boolean field
      implementation_note: >
        Persist as a nullable or defaulted boolean on the user preferences
        record. Default should be true (opted in) unless specified otherwise.

    - decision: transactional_emails_enabled validation happens at the API layer
      implementation_note: >
        If the request body contains transactional_emails_enabled: false, return
        HTTP 403 with error code TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED before any
        write occurs. Do not persist a partial update then reject — reject first.

  acceptance_criteria:
    - criterion: Marketing opt-out
      given: A user with marketing_emails_enabled = true
      when: PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences with { "marketing_emails_enabled": false }
      then: 200 returned, marketing_emails_enabled persisted as false

    - criterion: Transactional opt-out blocked
      given: Any user regardless of current preferences
      when: PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences with { "transactional_emails_enabled": false }
      then: >
        403 returned with body containing error code
        TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED; no write occurs

    - criterion: Transactional field absent — no-op
      given: A valid user
      when: PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences with only marketing fields
      then: transactional_emails_enabled is unchanged; request succeeds

  terminology_alignment:
    - domain_term: "transactional emails"
      technical_term: transactional_emails_enabled
      definition: >
        Emails required for system function — password resets, security alerts.
        Legally mandated to remain enabled. Maps to the boolean field
        transactional_emails_enabled in the preferences payload.

    - domain_term: "marketing emails"
      technical_term: marketing_emails_enabled
      definition: >
        Promotional or non-essential communications. User may disable at will.
        Maps to the boolean field marketing_emails_enabled in the preferences
        payload.

  contradictions_resolved:
    - conflict: >
        Bird v1 included AC2 allowing transactional email opt-out. Bird v2
        removed AC2 and added AC3 explicitly prohibiting it. These two versions
        directly contradict each other.
      resolution: >
        v2 supersedes v1 in full. v2 was submitted after legal review; it is
        the authoritative source. Shaq should implement only v2 behavior — no
        opt-out path for transactional emails exists.

  open_questions:
    - >
      What is the default value of marketing_emails_enabled for new users?
      Not specified by Bird or MJ. Assume true (opted in) unless product
      confirms otherwise.
    - >
      Should the 403 response body follow an existing error envelope format
      in the API, or is TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED a freestanding
      error code? Check existing 403 responses in the codebase for convention.
    - >
      Are there other notification channels (SMS, push) in scope for this
      endpoint, or is this strictly email? Not addressed by either Bird or MJ.
```

**Key notes for Shaq:**

The only live conflict in the inputs was Bird v1 vs v2 — resolved in favor of v2. MJ's architecture is fully consistent with Bird v2; no cross-agent contradictions remain.

The two open questions (default opt-in state for new users, error envelope format) are low-risk but worth a quick check before writing tests — the first affects your seed/migration script, the second affects your error serializer.
